specification.topic-statement: The HTTP completion analyser categorises finished curl requests by HTTP status code, constructs completion descriptors from response bodies and curl error states, and dispatches them to caller-supplied callbacks.

scope.in-scope: HTTP status code categorisation, completion descriptor construction for successful and failed requests, callback invocation, and bulk cancellation without callback dispatch.
scope.boundary: The multiplexer drives curl's multi-handle event loop and detects when individual transfers complete. This topic handles the analysis that follows completion detection.

The completion callback supplied by each provider receives the descriptor and is responsible for parsing the response body, updating provider state, and emitting stream events. Provider response parsing is out of scope here.

field.name: category
field.note: SUCCESS (HTTP 200-299), CLIENT_ERROR (400-499), SERVER_ERROR (500-599), or NETWORK_ERROR (all other HTTP codes or curl failures)

field.name: http_code
field.note: HTTP status code; zero for network-level errors

field.name: body
field.note: response body for successful transfers; null for network errors

field.name: error_message
field.note: human-readable description for network errors; null for HTTP responses

behavior.status-categorisation: Completed HTTP responses are classified by status code: - 200–299 → SUCCESS - 400–499 → CLIENT_ERROR - 500–599 → SERVER_ERROR - All other codes (including 0 for network errors) → NETWORK_ERROR
behavior.successful-completion: When a transfer completes with a non-zero HTTP status code, the completion descriptor is populated with the status code, the categorisation above, and the accumulated response body. The curl easy handle is removed from the multi handle, headers and the easy handle are freed, and the slot in the active request array is cleared. The caller's completion callback is then invoked with the descriptor.
behavior.network-error-completion: When curl itself reports an error (the CURLcode is not CURLE_OK), no HTTP status code is available. The completion descriptor is populated with HTTP code zero, category NETWORK_ERROR, no body, and an error message derived from curl's error description for that code. Cleanup and callback invocation proceed identically to successful completion.
behavior.bulk-cancellation: When all active requests must be abandoned (for example, on shutdown), each active slot is cleaned up — the easy handle is removed from the multi handle, headers are freed, and the slot is cleared — without invoking any completion callback. This terminates in-flight transfers silently.
